title: Check out every major smarphone launch in May including Vivo X300 Ultra, Oppo X9 Ultra, and OnePlus Nord CE 6
snippet: India will see many powerful smartphone launches in May from Vivo, OnePlus, and Oppo. Among the top launches this month will include the OnePlus Nord CE 6, Vivo X300 Ultra and Oppo X9 Ultra

title: AI is now outperforming human doctors in the emergency room diagnoses, new Harvard study reveals
snippet: A Harvard study reveals advanced AI models, like OpenAI's o1, can outperform doctors in diagnosing patients and planning clinical management, particularly in emergency scenarios.&nbsp;

title: Amazon Great Indian Summer Sale announced: bank offers, dates and top discounts
snippet: Amazon has confirmed its Great Indian Summer sale starting May 8. The company has also confirmed discounts on various categories, including smartphones and appliances.&nbsp;

title: iOS 27 releasing soon: Compatible iPhones, massive Siri overhaul, and deep AI camera integration
snippet: Apple will unveil iOS 27 at the WWDC keynote in September. The update is expected to focus on performance improvements, a significant Siri upgrade, and new features like AI enhancements in the camera app and photo editing tools.&nbsp;

title: OnePlus Nord CE6, CE 6 Lite India launch soon: Expected price, specs and features
snippet: OnePlus will launch the Nord CE6 and CE 6 Lite in India on May 7. Ahead of the launch, the company has revealed that the CE 6 will feature a 6.78-inch AMOLED display, Snapdragon 7s Gen 4, and 8,000mAh battery, while the CE 6 Lite will come with a 6.72-inch display and MediaTek Dimensity 7400.

title: Fujifilm Instax Mini 13 Review: A well-rounded entry-level instant camera
snippet: An entry-level instant camera that keeps things simple, now with a self-timer that actually makes it more practical to use.

title: OpenAI ignored employee pleas to report a violent ChatGPT user months before a deadly mass shooting
snippet: Amid lawsuits, a new report has highlighted employee's concerns about OpenAI's reluctance to report violent ChatGPT users to authorities.

title: Sam Altman invites Elon Musk to GPT-5.5 party amid $134 billion legal battle because ‘world needs more love’
snippet: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman has invited Elon Musk to the GPT-5.5 launch party amid the high profile lawsuit. Altman while handing out the invite on a social media post noted ‘world needs more love’

Meta faces New Mexico trial that could force changes to Facebook, other platforms
snippet: A New Mexico trial could force Meta to overhaul its social media platforms. The state accuses Meta of addicting young users and failing to protect children. A judge may order sweeping changes, potentially leading Meta to leave the state.

title: North Korea calls US cyber crime accusations 'absurd slander'
snippet: North Korea has vehemently denied US claims of cybercrimes funding its weapons programs, labeling them 'absurd slander.' Washington alleges Pyongyang has stolen billions in virtual assets, a crucial revenue stream amid sanctions.  The US Justice Department has prosecuted individuals involved in this

title: Saudi AI firm Humain launches enterprise AI initiative with Amazon Web Services
snippet: Saudi Arabian AI firm Humain is expanding its partnership with Amazon Web Services. This collaboration introduces Humain One, a new generative AI enterprise operating system.

title: GameStop makes $56 billion takeover bid for eBay
snippet: GameStop has launched a $55.5 billion takeover bid for eBay, aiming to transform the online marketplace into a formidable competitor to Amazon. The offer, valued at $125 per share, includes a mix of stock and cash, representing a significant premium.

title: Samsung Electronics appoints new TV chief amid mounting competition
snippet: Samsung Electronics has appointed a new head for its TV division. This leadership change comes as the company faces intense competition from Chinese manufacturers. The new head is expected to bring new ideas to the TV business. Samsung's TV profits have recently declined due to market challenges.

title: Anthropic nears $1.5 billion joint venture with Wall Street firms
snippet: AI firm Anthropic is reportedly close to a significant $1.5 billion joint venture, with major Wall Street players like Blackstone and Hellman & Friedman anchoring the investment.

title: UpGrad-Unacademy deal closes; India’s largest private satellite lifts off
snippet: Happy Monday! Upgrad is set to acquire Unacademy at a 90% markdown of its peak valuation. This and more in today's ETtech Morning Dispatch.

title: Skyroot Vikram-1 rocket is raring to soar high
snippet: Skyroot Aerospace is on the cusp of launching India's first privately built orbital rocket, Vikram-1, from Sriharikota within weeks. title: Tata Electronics aims to be $30 billion business with fab play: CEO & MD Randhir Thakur
snippet: Tata Electronics is rapidly expanding its semiconductor and electronics manufacturing operations. The company has achieved significant revenue growth and is now profitable. title: IT's revenue per employee up in productivity win
snippet: India's leading IT firms are seeing revenue per employee rise. This indicates a shift towards AI and automation driving productivity.  Companies like Tata Consultancy Services, Infosys, HCLTech, and Tech Mahindra reported growth.  This trend suggests a move away from headcount-based revenue models.

title: A new US phone network for Christians aims to block porn and gender-related content
snippet: A new US-wide cell phone network marketed to Christians is set to launch next week. It blocks porn, which experts in network security say marks the first time a US cell plan has used network-level blocking for such content that can’t be turned off even by adult account owners.
